company.name: Playtika
company.notes: Gaming (public PLTK). UA-creative + analyst angle. You hate gaming.

research.status_summary: Actively restructuring post-layoffs: Playtika completed its 6th round of layoffs in Jan 2026 (cutting ~500 employees, 15% of workforce) and subsidiary Wooga conducted another small round in May 2026; hiring is minimal and highly selective (~8-90 open roles visible externally).
research.hiring_policy: No public visa sponsorship or EOR policy stated; open roles are primarily Israel-HQ and studio-specific (Berlin/Kyiv/etc.) with no evidence of remote-first or EOR-friendly hiring — Glassdoor lists only 7-8 open positions globally as of Jun 2026.
recent_news: Jan 14, 2026 — Playtika announced its 6th round of layoffs since 2022, cutting 500 employees (15% of global workforce) and filing an SEC 8-K disclosing $12–15M in restructuring costs; CEO Antokol cited AI/automation as the replacement strategy ('do more with less').

May 7, 2026 — Q1 2026 earnings: revenue $744.7M (+5.5% YoY), driven by Disney Solitaire ($123.3M, +72.1% sequentially) and record DTC revenue of $291.8M (+62.8% YoY); net loss of $57.5M due to $461M SuperPlay earn-out; dividend suspended; full-year guidance raised to $2.75–2.85B.

May 14, 2026 — Playtika-owned Berlin studio Wooga completed yet another small round of layoffs (~12 roles across design, art, QA), with ex-staff publicly citing 'abysmal management decisions'; Wooga's June's Journey remains a top performer at $76M/quarter.
research.growth_signal: Headcount is shrinking (from ~3,500 toward ~3,000) while revenue is recovering (+5.5% YoY in Q1 2026), but the company is betting growth on AI-driven UA, the SuperPlay/Disney Solitaire franchise, and a DTC platform now approaching a $1.2B annual run rate — not on hiring.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit: Playtika is explicitly replacing human UA and creative teams with AI/automation, making it a cautionary case study rather than an employer — useful as a 'UA-creative + AI displacement' analyst angle or competitive intelligence reference, but not a live opportunity given frozen hiring and no EOR posture.
